lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The clock strikes at 9 AM, and the Psychiatrist is preparing her session.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Within a few minutes, Elsa has arrived.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Good morning, Elsa! Here's your monthly prescription.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Good morning, Miss... and thank you.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Are you feeling well? Any strange dreams again?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I am... alright. No dreams last night... just cold and... empty.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: That's good to hear. Usually your dreams would incite violent reactions towards yourself.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: What about your recent thoughts? Have they been disturbing you?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I’m... not sure. I don’t want to think about it that much.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Well, I'm always here to help. In the end, change still lies within you.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: I know that your past has traumatized you, but we can keep working together to solve this problem. Okay?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ... Thank you.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I will... take my leave now.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Be safe now, dear.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Before heading home, Elsa decided to visit the Church.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Where she look around the area and notices the confessional booth.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She quietly stepped into one of the confession booths to speak with the Priest.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Father... I have a confession to make...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: What troubles you today, my child?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I feel like... God has abandoned me.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Oh, my child... My dear child of God... God will never abandon you, and you know that.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: You must never let your faith be shaken by evil. Always walk the path of light.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: No matter how many things the Devil says, Always remember that God will protect you.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ... Thank you, Father. I knew I could rely on you.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I hope this darkness in my heart disappears soon.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: May God be with you, my child.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: On her way home, she felt a sudden heavy feeling pressing against her chest.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: My head's getting hazy... Was it the Pills?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: No... I hope its not...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I... I need to rest...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Thud!

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She heard a noise in the living room...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Hesitantly, she approached the living room door...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Her body froze as her eyes widened, staring at the undulating, and twisted yet familiar figure.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: M-Mom!?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Mom, is that you?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: No... No, that can't be real... That can't be her...

choices.text: Take pills to calm her mind

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She shakes her head and checks her pockets. She takes the pills and forces them down. The vision fades slowly.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She closes her eyes and begins to pray. Her words tremble as the vision begins to fade.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: W-what was that...? The devil's trick?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I hope it's not...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Ugh, I'm exhausted. I really need some rest...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Before heading to bed, she kneels down beside the bed and prays

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Lord, If you're there... I've come to ask for forgiveness...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Whether it's my Parents' sin for leaving me in this house, or my Sister went missing from their abandonment...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Please guide my Sister to a bright path, wash me away from my sins...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: And Lord... I hope that you'll forgive my Family's sins...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: In the name of the Father, Son and of the Holy Spirit...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Amen...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Feeling exhausted from earlier, Elsa head to bed and fell asleep, drifting into one of her dreams...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As she slowly drifted into her dreams, It was just pitch black void, stretching endlessly.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Until she suddenly stumbled upon a familiar statue.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: What...? What is this? Where am I?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She noticed a silhouette in the distance...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Hello...? Who's out there?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As the figure slowly emerged from the darkness, she recognized the familiar outline...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ... Dad?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Dad? Is that you?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Dad? If that's you... Why did you leave us?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Why did you leave us? Is our family not enough for you?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: What happened to you and Mom? Did you even hurt Mom!? Did she do something bad to you?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Did you even know where my Sister went? Is she still alive???

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I need to know, Dad. Why did you left us to die!?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I need to know...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Answer me, Dad...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Answer me... Please...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ANSWER ME!!

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: DAD!!!

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Elsa checked the time and it was 8 in the Morning.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She took a breather first, whilst thinking her thoughts about the dream she had experienced.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She sits back up in bed and wonders at the window...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: It’s a new day... I wonder why my Dad's in that dream...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: There has to be a reason...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I need answers...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Elsa returned to her appointment once again.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The Psychiatrist noticed her and approached calmly.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Good morning, Elsa. Here for another session for today?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Not exactly, but I do want to ask something.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Oh? Go ahead. I'm all ears.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...Did God took my Dad away?

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: God has... different plans for all of us, dear.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: I'm not sure about your father though.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: You did told me in the previous sessions that he left you and your family right?

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: I'm sure God has plans for him in the end.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...I suppose that's true...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: But it's still hard to accept that he wronged us... That he left me and my family behind.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Suddenly, a heavy feeling settles over the room.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Your father never wanted to see us, Elsa. Pray harder to will his ignorance away.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Head back home and you'll find your answers there...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I understand...

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Hmm? You were saying something, dear?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Huh? Oh.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I-it's nothing.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Hmm... Well if you came here for your prescription pill refill, here.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The Psychiatrist gave her another set of pills. Use it wisely.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: There we go. I hope this will ease your current situation.

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Since we're trying to fix your problem and all.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ... Thanks.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I'll uhh... Head back home now...

lines.speaker: Psychiatrist
lines.text: Ok. Stay safe, dear.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Elsa returned to the Church and gave her prayers.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As she quietly entered one of the confession booths to speak with the Priest.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Father... I have a question...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Ask forth, my child. I'm listening.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...Did God took my Dad away?

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Oh, my child... Perhaps your father was never part of His plan.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: And God... has other plans.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...I suppose that's true...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: But it’s still hard to accept that he abandoned me and my family...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Suddenly, a strange weight hangs in the air.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Your father never wanted to see us, Elsa. Your prayers won’t bring him back.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Head back home and you'll find your answers there...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I understand...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Oh? Did you mention something, My child?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Oh! Uhh...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: It's nothing, Father...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Hmm... Well, if it's about your problem, Just remember about our talk yesterday, ok?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I... I will, father...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: And thank you...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: May God forgive your sins, young one...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Back at home, Elsa decided to look around the house due to the current dream she experienced last night.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: While looking back at the living room, She noticed that the hanging figure wasn't there

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Phew... Thank goodness she's not here...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Huh? What’s that?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She saw a suspicious underneath a pile of papers tucked in the cabinet.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: A warrant arrest of the father abused the mother and her children, which lead to the father's arrest in a nearby location.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: A warrant? Wait... Is this what Mom meant?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: If it does... Then what is Mom telling me?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Wait... this address... A prison? Dad’s somewhere in ***** City?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Maybe Mom wants me to head there?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I do need some answers after all...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ... I wonder if he really is what I feared...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: A monster.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: End of Chapter 1.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: No... God would want me to move on.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: He will no longer belongs in my life.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: And so, she burned the warrant.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The last physical trace of her father. Reduced to ash.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As she stare to the burning warrant to ashes, she continued to search more answers in her home...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: End of Chapter 1.
